campaigns.context: A ClickFix social engineering campaign delivers Vidar stealer payloads through staged batch scripts, ZIP archives, and HTA files hosted across more than 20 domains, with ConnectWise Control client binaries also present in the payload infrastructure. Victims are directed to fake browser verification pages that inject clipboard commands; payload staging paths follow consistent naming conventions including N.zip, N_drv.bat, N_smb.bat, and N_ftp.bat. Six researchers reported 96 IOCs spanning July 18 to July 23.
campaigns.name: ClickFix AiTM phishing via Russian-domain infrastructure

campaigns.context: AsyncRAT and DCrat payloads communicate via C2 nodes distributed across multiple free DDNS providers including dynuddns.com, bumbleshrimp.com, ddnsguru.com, and 1cooldns.com, alongside a shared listener at 157.10.53.213 on ports 443 and 8080. A World Cup 2026-themed lure domain (keobongdaworldcup2026.com) is also associated, consistent with social engineering timed to high-traffic events. Two researchers reported 95 IOCs between July 20 and July 23.
campaigns.name: AsyncRAT and DCrat C2 cluster abusing free DDNS providers

campaigns.context: A single domain, lestrani.de, hosts subdomains impersonating at least five European organizations: Agenzia delle Entrate (Italian tax authority), ANTAI amendes (French road fines authority), Elster/Finanzamt (German tax portal), Commerzbank (German bank), and AXA insurance. The multi-country, multi-brand abuse of a single base domain is consistent with a phishing kit deployed by one actor running parallel credential-harvest pages targeting European victims across different national contexts. One researcher reported 18 IOCs on July 20.
campaigns.name: Multi-brand European gov and bank phishing on lestrani.de

campaigns.context: An Android malware cluster uses duckdns.org dynamic DNS subdomains and attacker-controlled GitHub Pages alongside .top domains for command-and-control communication. Associated MD5 and SHA-256 hashes indicate multiple samples in active circulation across the campaign. Two researchers reported 16 IOCs on July 22.
campaigns.name: Android malware cluster using duckdns and GitHub Pages for C2

campaigns.context: The domain dropboxx.us, a double-x typosquat of dropbox.com, hosts a mail subdomain alongside multiple randomly-named subdomains consistent with per-victim tracking links used in credential-harvesting campaigns. The infrastructure pattern - a branded typosquat with a mail endpoint and generated random subdomains - is consistent with a phishing kit targeting Dropbox users via email lures. One researcher reported 16 IOCs on July 23.
campaigns.name: Dropbox credential phishing via dropboxx.us typosquat domain

campaigns.context: Attackers deliver ConnectWise ScreenConnect client MSI installers via legitimate cloud relay subdomains on screenconnect.com and attacker-controlled domains including lockalcontractiongesatate.cc, alongside compromised WordPress sites at ijexgroup.com.br and tomballtxplumbers.com. The MSI installers grant persistent remote access under the guise of legitimate remote support software. Three reporters flagged 14 combined IOCs between July 18 and July 23.
campaigns.name: ScreenConnect remote access C2 across multiple lure domains

campaigns.context: A Magecart campaign uses relay subdomains on cloudservicecon.com, cdn-statmetrics.com, dinkfoundry.com, and infolservice.com to exfiltrate payment card data from compromised e-commerce sites, with faketools.click also associated with the cluster. The relay subdomain naming mimics legitimate CDN and analytics services to blend into normal web traffic. Two researchers reported 10 IOCs on July 22.
campaigns.name: Magecart payment skimming relay infrastructure

campaigns.context: The domain heliumcompliance.info and its review subdomain serve malware from short path identifiers including trailing dash and single-character variants, with both HTTP and HTTPS variants active. The compliance-themed domain name is consistent with a social engineering lure presenting a fake review or compliance requirement to prompt victim interaction. Two researchers reported 8 IOCs on July 22.
campaigns.name: Malware distribution via heliumcompliance.info compliance lure

campaigns.context: Two domains impersonating Tropical Smoothie Cafe - tropicalsmotiecafe.com and order-tropicalismoothecaffe.com - host order-themed credential-harvesting pages, with the latter also carrying a redundant order-prefix subdomain. Both were reported by the same researcher on July 21, suggesting a single actor registering multiple typosquat variants simultaneously. One researcher reported 8 combined IOCs on July 21.
campaigns.name: Tropical Smoothie Cafe brand phishing via typosquat domains

campaigns.context: The domain eznamsk.com hosts a phishing endpoint at the /sk/ path alongside a matomo.eznamsk.com analytics subdomain, indicating active monitoring of victim visits using open-source web analytics. The combination of a regional path identifier and embedded analytics is consistent with a targeted credential-harvesting operation tracking visitor behavior in real time. Two researchers reported 8 IOCs between July 21 and July 22.
campaigns.name: Credential phishing with analytics tracking at eznamsk.com

campaigns.context: Four domains - skjwoodstone.com, tonghedianzi.com, yijiatuliao.com, and zhuodashufa.com - host phishing pages at the /safety/update/ path, consistent with a single kit deployed across multiple hosts using a shared path as a campaign marker. Domain names appear to be transliterations of Chinese business terms, suggesting Chinese-registered infrastructure behind the campaign. One researcher reported 8 IOCs on July 22.
campaigns.name: Safety-page phishing kit deployed across four .com domains

campaigns.context: Phishing pages impersonating Resona Bank appear across multiple domains sharing a /login path, including teamswedenclub.com, workinghardo.link, and vpn.rujun.xyz, alongside a direct IP listener at 79.76.52.42:3333. The shared path pattern and consistent endpoint structure across geographically diverse hosts suggest a single kit operator running parallel credential-harvest pages. Four researchers reported 7 IOCs between July 20 and July 22.
campaigns.name: Japanese bank phishing targeting Resona Bank via /login pages

campaigns.context: Remcos RAT is delivered via an HTA file with an unusually long descriptive filename designed to obscure the payload type, staged on an IP-based host and distributed through redirect URLs on fasthouse.me. Supporting payload URLs on zynimg.com and usafansweepstakes2026.us serve additional stages under image-hosting-style paths. One researcher reported 7 IOCs on July 21.
campaigns.name: Remcos RAT delivery via HTA loader with image-host staging

campaigns.context: Multiple attacker-controlled Cloudflare Tunnel subdomains on trycloudflare.com expose WsgiDAV or generic open directory listings used to stage and serve malware. The use of trycloudflare.com provides HTTPS coverage and bypasses domain-reputation controls by routing through trusted Cloudflare infrastructure. Two reporters flagged 7 IOCs across three tunnel hostnames between July 20 and July 22.
campaigns.name: Malware staging via WsgiDAV open directories on trycloudflare tunnels

campaigns.context: Domains endl.site and global-research.space expose PHP redirect or gate endpoints following an /advN.php naming pattern across multiple subdomains including docsmanagement and axes on endl.site. The structured PHP path pattern and involvement of abuse_ch as a reporter suggest this infrastructure functions as a traffic distribution system or redirect layer within a malware delivery chain. Three researchers including abuse_ch reported 6 IOCs between July 22 and July 23.
campaigns.name: Traffic distribution infrastructure using adv*.php endpoints on endl.site

campaigns.context: The domain datusha.com.cn communicates on non-standard ports 6685 and 6698, a pattern consistent with malware C2 using custom port configurations to evade default traffic monitoring. Two reporters flagged 6 IOCs on July 21 but no specific malware family was identified in the available data.
campaigns.name: Chinese domain C2 on custom ports at datusha.com.cn

campaigns.context: The domain alwoodleybuildinganddevelopments.co.uk, a legitimate UK construction company site, hosts three subdomains whose names are full 40-character SHA-1 hashes, a distinctive pattern associated with bot C2 frameworks that use DNS as a covert channel. No malware family tag is available from the data, but the infrastructure pattern is consistent with a compromised domain repurposed for C2 communication. One researcher reported 6 IOCs on July 21.
campaigns.name: SHA-1 hash subdomains on compromised UK construction domain

campaigns.context: The domain reha-stims.com hosts subdomains impersonating webmail portals for mzkt.by, a Belarusian heavy vehicle manufacturer, and customs.go.kr, the South Korean customs authority. The multi-country, cross-sector targeting on a single base domain suggests a shared phishing service or kit operator running parallel credential-harvesting pages. One researcher reported 6 IOCs on July 22.
campaigns.name: Cross-country webmail phishing on reha-stims.com

campaigns.context: The wp-admin directory of martincristal.com.ar, an apparently compromised Argentine business site, hosts a PHP download script and ZIP payload within a /wp-admin/JCP/ path used for malware staging. Abusing a legitimate site's WordPress admin path is a common technique to exploit existing domain reputation for payload delivery. Two researchers reported 5 IOCs on July 22.
campaigns.name: Payload delivery via compromised WordPress admin path at martincristal.com.ar

campaigns.context: Several GitHub repositories and user accounts are flagged for hosting or distributing malware, including a raw ZIP download from the dm-7926/dz-nvd repository and accounts using the handles business-49 and sean100496. The use of legitimate GitHub infrastructure for malware staging is a common technique to bypass URL reputation filtering and security product blocks. Four researchers reported 4 IOCs between July 18 and July 23.
campaigns.name: Malware delivery via attacker-controlled GitHub repositories

campaigns.context: The domain udnvk.site and its associated IP 27.102.137.59 are attributed to Kimsuky, a North Korean state-sponsored threat actor known for spear-phishing and intelligence-collection campaigns targeting government, defense, and research organizations. A single researcher reported 4 IOCs on July 23; the attribution rests on that researcher's analysis and is not corroborated by additional reporters.
campaigns.name: Kimsuky APT C2 node at udnvk.site

campaigns.context: A subdomain of assistenza-spa.com named 'in-attesa-della-conferma-di-nexi' (Italian for 'waiting for Nexi confirmation') hosts a credential-harvesting page impersonating Nexi, Italy's leading payment processing company. The Italian-language subdomain targets consumers awaiting transaction approval notifications. One researcher reported 4 IOCs on July 21.
campaigns.name: Nexi payment phishing via assistenza-spa.com subdomain

campaigns.context: The domain extensionscover.xyz serves a NetLock RMM Agent installer for 64-bit Windows alongside a PowerShell setup script, following a pattern consistent with malicious RMM tool deployment for unauthorized remote access. The domain name is designed to suggest browser extension infrastructure, likely used as a social engineering lure to prompt installation. Two researchers flagged 4 IOCs on July 20.
campaigns.name: NetLock RMM agent delivery via extensionscover.xyz

campaigns.context: An Njrat C2 node at 193.161.193.99 is mapped through a portmap.host dynamic DNS subdomain that encodes an attacker handle and port number in the hostname (ahmeddoserr-37232). Portmap.host is a free port-forwarding service frequently abused by RAT operators to obscure IP attribution and maintain C2 availability across IP changes. One researcher reported 4 IOCs on July 20.
campaigns.name: Njrat C2 via portmap.host dynamic DNS tunnel
